cards.title: Hugging Face Discloses Agentic AI-Driven Intrusion via Malicious Dataset Loader
cards.why_it_matters: This is the first publicly disclosed breach where an autonomous LLM-based attacker framework executed tens of thousands of actions against a major AI platform, with direct implications for how you think about dataset supply-chain security and agentic threat models.
cards.summary: An attacker exploited two dataset-processing code-execution paths (a remote-code loader and a template-injection flaw) to reach node-level access, harvest credentials, and move laterally across internal clusters. The campaign used an autonomous agent framework running thousands of actions across short-lived sandboxes. Hugging Face used open-weight model GLM 5.2 on-prem for forensic log analysis after frontier API providers blocked attack-payload submissions via safety guardrails. No public models, datasets, or Spaces were tampered with; affected credentials were rotated.

cards.title: NVIDIA NeMo Automodel + Hugging Face Diffusers: Distributed Diffusion Fine-Tuning at Any Scale
cards.why_it_matters: As an AI/ML engineer working with diffusion models, this gives you a production-grade, Apache 2.0 training stack that scales from one GPU to hundreds without checkpoint conversion or model rewrites.
cards.summary: NVIDIA and Hugging Face have integrated NeMo Automodel — a PyTorch DTensor-native library — with the Diffusers ecosystem. It supports FSDP2, tensor, context, and pipeline parallelism via YAML config, not code rewrites. Both full fine-tuning and LoRA PEFT are supported. Flow-matching models (FLUX.1-dev, Wan 2.1, HunyuanVideo) are included; checkpoints round-trip cleanly back into DiffusionPipeline.
